text: The widespread use of transition metals in atom transfer radical polymerization (ATRP) continues to be limited by contamination of produced materials, which is especially troublesome in the production of polymers and copolymers for use as components of materials that must be free from heavy metal contaminants. To address this critical challenge, we report a new, metal-free photoinitiator based on an organic small molecule that acts as a general catalyst for the photoinitiated ATRP of vinyl monomers in what we describe as “light activated” or “LA”-ATRP. Using the photoinitiator, we have control over the molecular weight, polydispersity index (PDI) and end group functionality, which enables the straightforward synthesis of a wide range of block copolymers, as well as being compatible with other controlled radical polymerization (CRP) methods, thus significantly increasing the structural diversity and the range of applications for this class of materials. The organic photoredox catalysts we utilize provide a versatile platform of tools that can be employed for both the synthesis of small molecules and polymers, while also providing a contaminant-free method of polymerization and copolymerization that can obviate the need for metal contaminants in the materials produced.
